summary: Atomic close-and-record: close commits .todo/ itself to kill the phantom-commit hazard
description: close currently writes the ticket file via writeTicket() but never commits .todo/; the skill prose tells the user to run git add .todo/ && git commit afterward. A failed close followed by a manual commit produces a phantom commit whose message says close over a still-open ticket. Make close stage and optionally commit .todo/ itself (e.g. --commit-state, default in managed mode) so close-and-record is atomic and the manual step cannot desync. P0 / highest priority: this is a correctness fix, not just ergonomics. File: src/commands/close.ts.
